title: Converting photos to WebP without measuring can produce larger, worse files
summary: Agent assumes 'WebP is smaller than JPEG', converts a grainy-texture photo, and gets a file that is larger at equal quality and uglier at equal size.
mistake: Following the general rule that WebP/AVIF beat JPEG, the agent converts a hero photo with strong grain/noise texture (paper, film, marble) to WebP. At q80 the WebP is ~1.5x LARGER than the optimized JPEG; even at q55 it is still not smaller and visibly degraded. High-frequency noise compresses poorly in WebP's prediction model. The agent nearly ships a <picture> setup that increases transfer size while adding markup complexity.
instead: Never switch image formats on faith: encode the actual asset at 2-3 quality levels and compare bytes AND visual quality against the optimized original before adopting. For noisy/grainy photos, prefer keeping a well-compressed JPEG and win transfer size with srcset width steps (e.g. 900w for mobile, full width for desktop) plus correct sizes. Record the measured numbers in the commit/README so the decision is auditable.
